dictionary_sections.definition: RecursiveToroidalEncoding is a TZPID dictionary-fill candidate anchored by the canonical relation T = S1 \times S1.. It is not promoted content; it fills the currently empty dictionary slots from the folder's existing equation state and linked context.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: In context, RecursiveToroidalEncoding names the operation or constraint expressed by the recovered equation. Source context: on and the quantum control architecture of the DAANS sphere — through registry entries ID 1121 to 1135. --- ## Part One: The 153,600 Mapping Hypothesis (ID 1121) ### The Channel Count The human genome contains approximately $10^9$ base pairs. The average size of a functional regulatory unit — a coherent region of chromatin that operates as a single regulatory module — is approximately $6.5 \times 10^3$ base pairs (6.5 kilobases, or 6.5 kb). The biological segmentation heuristic (ID 1121): $$N_\text{seg} \approx \frac{L_\text{genome}}{L_\text{unit}} = \frac{10^9}{6.51 \times 10^3} \approx 153{,}600$$ **The genome, segmented by its own functional logic, produces 153,600 coherent quantum channels.** This number is not chosen for numerological reasons.
